data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-16T01:43:22.320Z</current_datetime>

I have this MSTest data-driven test using DynamicData with object arrays. Is there a more type-safe way to write this in modern MSTest?

```csharp
[TestMethod]
[DynamicData(nameof(TestData))]
public void Validate_ReturnsExpected(string input, bool expected)
{
    var result = Validator.IsValid(input);
    Assert.AreEqual(expected, result);
}

public static IEnumerable<object[]> TestData =>
[
    ["valid@email.com", true],
    ["invalid", false],
    ["", false],
];
```

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a more type-safe way to write MSTest data-driven tests instead of using `DynamicData` with `object[]`. This is a knowledge question about modern MSTest features.

Modern MSTest (v3.3+) introduced typed test data sources. Specifically, they can use `DynamicData` with tuples or use the newer `[TestDataSource]` attribute pattern. But the most relevant improvement is that MSTest v3.3+ supports `IEnumerable<(T1, T2)>` (tuples) with `DynamicData`, and MSTest 3.4+ introduced `[AutoDataSource]` or similar.


The key improvement is that starting with MSTest 3.3, you can use tuple-based `DynamicData` with `IEnumerable<(T1, T2, ...)>` instead of `IEnumerable<object[]>`, which provides compile-time type safety for your test parameters.
